system_description.overview: Delve Technologies Inc. provides an AI-automated compliance platform designed to help companies achieve and maintain compliance with frameworks including SOC 2, HIPAA, GDPR, ISO 27001, and PCI DSS. The platform leverages AI to automate evidence gathering, perform continuous compliance monitoring, and includes AI code scanning, infrastructure monitoring, automated security questionnaire completion, and access control management.

products.Automated Compliance Management Platform: End-to-end compliance automation for SOC 2, HIPAA, ISO 27001, GDPR, PCI DSS, and more; integration with client existing tech stack; continuous compliance monitoring and gap identification; automated evidence collection.
products.AI-Powered Security and Compliance Featu: Automated code scanning (review of code pushes for security vulnerabilities), automated infrastructure scanning (continuous monitoring), access control monitoring (detection and alerts for unauthorized access), security questionnaire automation.
products.Compliance Support Services: Compliance implementation services including onboarding, setup, and framework implementation; ongoing compliance support including expert guidance and auditor coordination.

infrastructure.cloud_provider: Microsoft Azure (carve-out — Azure data center controls excluded from scope)
regions: Not specified — Azure regions not named in report
infrastructure.physical_presence: None — no physical offices; all data processed on cloud services
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ — report explicitly states 'high availability data centers with multiple availability zones'; DR plan references spinning up multiple servers across all availability zones in Azure

network_architecture.network_segmentation: System hosted in Microsoft Azure in a virtual private cloud (VPC) environment with segmented VPCs and access control lists (ACLs). Network segmentation implemented to separate systems and data based on sensitivity and criticality.
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols and services. Firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management. Network firewalls implemented, configured, and managed to control and monitor incoming and outgoing traffic.
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS deployed via Azure to identify and protect against threats. Intrusion detection system utilized to monitor and detect unauthorized access or anomalies within the network.
network_architecture.vpn: Remote access done through a bastion host with a virtual private network (VPN). VPC VPN connections shown in network diagram.
network_architecture.tls: TLS with certificates from established third-party certificate authority
network_architecture.content_filtering: All systems restricted to internet with content filtering system routed through proxy server
network_architecture.topology: Internet -> VPN/Internet Gateway -> VPC WEST and VPC EAST (multiple availability zones) with private subnets. VPC VPN Gateways connect via IPSec VPN tunnel. Prod HA, Dev, Test, D.com in VPC WEST; Prod HR in VPC EAST. Remote users connect via bastion host + VPN. NOTE: Network diagram in report is a sample/placeholder — instruction text overlay present saying 'Please replace this with your actual network diagram'.

compute: Azure virtual servers (cloud architecture including database, networking devices, virtual servers)
application_architecture.environments: Separate development, test, staging, and production environments. Changes tested in separate development or test environment before implementation. CC8.1.4 confirmed no exceptions.

databases: Not named — 'cloud architecture including database' mentioned generically
data_storage.backup_frequency: Daily — CC7.4.2 confirmed: 'Backups are taken daily, multi-availability zones are configured'

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: Azure Active Directory (passwords controlled through Password policy of Azure; Active Directory referenced for server and database accounts)
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for user accounts with administrative access to production platform; MFA required for source code tool access; MFA for email
authentication_access_control.access_revocation_sla: Within one business day upon termination
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Azure password policy: periodic forced changes, password expiry and complexity requirements; accounts disabled after limited unsuccessful logon attempts; guest and anonymous logins not allowed; unattended desktops locked on inactivity

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service used to encrypt data at rest
encryption.key_management: Azure KMS (cloud provider key management service) — access to production environment access keys restricted to authorized individuals

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Not named — 'source code management tool' referenced with MFA access
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Code repository branch rules configured to ensure every merge request to move change to production requires additional approval. Developers cannot make changes to production without additional approval.
ci_cd_devops.iac: Baseline configurations retained within infrastructure as code; configuration management tool used for implementation and rollback
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: CTO/Management set up automatic patch management; monthly patch management reviews; workstations configured for automatic updates and scanned daily for patch compliance

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Not named — 'monitoring tool' that generates alerts on predefined thresholds
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Not named — logging enabled for administrative activities, logon attempts, data deletions, changes to functions, security configurations, permissions, and roles
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured to notify IT management of high and critical risk events

third_party_services.purpose: Cloud infrastructure / data center services (subservice organization, carve-out)

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and Malware protection software installed on all production servers (continuous scanning, weekly definition updates) and Mac workstations/laptops (daily updates)
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications; internal IT Infrastructure team performs periodic scans
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annually by independent third-party; high/critical findings remediated in timely manner

untestable: CC2.2.3 — No significant changes to people/roles during engagement period
CC3.2.1 — No cyber incidents during engagement period (cybersecurity insurance control)
CC6.5.2 — No customer terminations during engagement period
CC7.3.1 — No security incidents reported during engagement period
excluded_criteria: CC6.4 — Azure responsible for physical security of data center
A1.2 — Delve does not have an office; environmental security managed by building management/Azure
Processing Integrity criteria — deemed not relevant to system
Privacy criteria — deemed not relevant to system
notable_controls: CC8.1.2 — Branch protection rules enforced; merge requires additional approval
CC8.1.3 — Source code changes logged, time-stamped, attributed in SCM tool with MFA access
CC7.4.2 — Daily backups confirmed; multi-AZ backup configuration confirmed
CC4.1.1 — Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
CC6.1.5 — Cloud provider KMS for encryption key management
CC2.1.1 — IDS utilized to monitor network for unauthorized access and anomalies

infrastructure.rationale: Azure multi-AZ confirmed, VPC with IDS/IPS, VPN, daily backups. No named regions, no CDN mentioned, no multi-region. Network diagram in report is a placeholder/sample — not the actual diagram. Single cloud provider.

application_architecture.rationale: Separate environments confirmed, branch protection confirmed, IaC referenced. However, no languages, frameworks, compute types (EC2-equivalent), API style, or CI/CD tool disclosed. Architecture pattern not stated.

data_layer.rationale: Daily backups confirmed, multi-AZ backups confirmed, data classification and retention policies confirmed, per-tenant segregation confirmed. No specific database technologies named, no caching layer disclosed, no data warehouse.

security_posture.rationale: Annual external pen test, monthly vuln scans, IDS/IPS, firewalls, MFA enforced, anti-virus/malware, cybersecurity insurance, quarterly access reviews, 1-business-day termination SLA, KMS encryption. No WAF named, no SIEM named.

devops_maturity.rationale: IaC referenced (configuration as code), branch protection confirmed, separate environments, emergency change process, monthly patch management. However no CI/CD tool named, no SCM platform named, no container orchestration mentioned.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy exists, annual review and testing confirmed, daily backups confirmed, multi-AZ failover confirmed. No RTO/RPO disclosed. No multi-region failover. DR plan spins up servers across AZs in Azure.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Only Azure explicitly named as subservice organization. No other third-party vendors disclosed in the report. Single critical cloud dependency with no named alternatives.

overall.rationale: Solid foundational controls for a compliance platform (ironic given product purpose). All 57 controls passed with no exceptions. However, the report contains numerous unfilled template placeholders including the network diagram (sample diagram shown), company address, and signing authority name, which significantly reduces confidence in report completeness. Very limited tech stack specificity.

red_flags: CRITICAL: Network diagram in report is explicitly a placeholder/sample — instruction overlay text says 'Please replace this with your actual network diagram'. Company submitted a template diagram, not their actual infrastructure.

CRITICAL: Multiple highlighted instruction blocks remain in the report body (yellow highlight with 'Instructions: Modify this text...') indicating this is a partially-completed Delve template used as their own report.
Signing authority name field is 'Your Name Here' — placeholder not completed.
No specific cloud services named beyond 'Azure' (no App Service, AKS, Azure SQL, etc.).
Only 3-month audit period (Jan-Apr 2025) — shorter than typical 12-month SOC 2 Type 2.
yellow_flags: No CI/CD tool named despite source code management being referenced.
No SIEM or log aggregation platform named.
Only one named subservice organization (Azure) — no other vendors disclosed despite being a SaaS platform with likely multiple dependencies.
Audit firm 'Accorp Partners' — less established than Big 4 or major SOC auditors.
Observation period only 3 months — not the standard 12 months for Type 2 reports.
green_flags: Zero exceptions across all 57 controls tested.
Annual external penetration testing by independent third party.
Multi-AZ deployment with daily backups confirmed.
MFA enforced for administrative and production access.
1-business-day access revocation SLA for terminated employees.
IaC used for infrastructure management with rollback capability.
Branch protection rules enforced with required approvals for production merges.
BCDR plan with annual testing confirmed.
Risk and Governance Executive Committee (RGEC) with semiannual meetings.
key_observations: This is Delve's own SOC 2 report generated using the Delve compliance platform — a meta-observation: the product being sold is the same tool used to generate this report. Quality of template completion is below standard.
The report was generated on 8/7/2025 based on management assertion date, but covers period Jan-Apr 2025 — 4-month lag.
Auditor license number 'PAC-FIRM-LIC-47383' shown on page 7 — verify if this is a real AICPA license.
Trust criteria of Security + Availability + Confidentiality is the standard 3-criteria bundle; Processing Integrity and Privacy explicitly excluded as 'not relevant'.
Physical security entirely carved out to Azure — acceptable for cloud-only companies.
The report structure (CC1 through CC9) follows the standard AICPA TSP 2017 framework.
